result.summary: FusionAuth exposes a tenant-hosted REST API, a local API MCP server, a docs MCP server, and an npm-delivered CLI; documented developer auth is primarily via FusionAuth API keys.
result.description: FusionAuth is a customer identity and access management platform for authentication, authorization, and user management. It can be self-hosted or run in FusionAuth Cloud and supports standards such as OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect.

fusionauth_api_key.label: FusionAuth API key
fusionauth_api_key.setup: In your FusionAuth instance, create an API key following [Authenticate with our APIs](https://fusionauth.io/docs/apis/authentication). Scope the key to the minimum permissions needed; the docs and MCP blog both recommend narrowly scoped keys. You use this key in the `Authorization` header for HTTP APIs, and tools such as the MCP server read it from `API_KEY_APIKEYAUTH`.

variables.name: api_base_url
variables.description: Base URL of the target FusionAuth instance, for example `http://localhost:9011`.
